WEAK LANDSPOUT TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN BRIEFLY, CAUSING MINOR DAMAGE TO TREES. WINDS ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 40-60MPH. PATH LENGTH OF AROUND 100 YARDS OR LESS, AND WIDTH OF AROUND 10 TO 20 YARDS IS ESTIMATED BASED ON SOCIAL MEDIA VIDEO FOOTAGE.

BRIEF TOUCHDOWN OF TORNADO THAT CAUSED DAMAGE TO SOME TREES IN THE PATH IN ADDITION TO THROWING AROUND A TRAMPOLINE, AND BLOWING OFF SEVERAL SHINGLES OF A NEARBY HOUSE. TORNADO LIKELY BEGAN AND ENDED IN A FIELD MAKING THE START AND END POINTS DIFFICULT TO DETERMINE. BASED ON DAMAGE, RADAR DATA, AND A PUBLIC REPORT, IT APPEARS THE TORNADO WAS AROUND 30 TO 50 YARDS WIDE AND TRAVELED AROUND 200 YARDS. DAMAGE SEEN DURING THE SURVEY IS CONSISTENT WITH WINDS AROUND 80 MPH.

MOST MAIN ROADS IN LAFOLLETTE CLOSED. LAFOLLETTE MIDDLE SCHOOL, FAITH WAY ASSEMBLY OF GOD CHURCH, AS WELL AS ANOTHER NEARBY PLACE OF WORSHIP WERE FLOODED. FIVE PEOPLE WERE EVACUATED BY BOAT ON HIGHWAY 63. POLICE AND FIRE STATIONS IN LAFOLLETTE WERE FLOODED. TEMPORARY SHELTERS WERE ESTABLISHED.
